title: Round highlights + player rail: text box looks rough; desktop swipe broken
description:
From GitHub issue #94

Two related UI issues:

1. **Round highlights carousel text box** — visually rough. Needs design love (padding, border, contrast).
2. **Player rail at top of the round-end / leaderboard** — same rough vibe.
3. **Swipe gesture on the highlights carousel does not work on desktop browsers.** Mobile not yet tested.

Files:
- \`components/round-highlights-carousel.tsx\`
- Player chip / rail rendering — \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` (LeaderboardRow ~L1989, header rail near L1340)

For desktop swipe: most likely the carousel is using \`touchstart\`/\`touchend\` only and no \`pointerdown\`/\`pointermove\` fallback. Switch to Pointer Events or wire arrow buttons + keyboard arrow-left/right.

Acceptance:
- Highlights box looks intentional (matches the game-card aesthetic)
- Player rail at top has consistent padding + chip styling
- Carousel responds to mouse drag AND arrow keys on desktop
- Mobile swipe still works (regression test)

title: 'Loading leaderboard…' on last round needs a countdown timer
description:
From GitHub issue #93

Earlier change (#76) replaced "Next round in Xs" with "Loading leaderboard…" on the final round's reveal. Without a visible countdown players don't know when the game-over screen lands — feels stuck.

Need: same countdown-pill UI as other rounds, but pointing at the same phase end and labeled "Leaderboard in Xs" or similar.

File: \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` — the reveal-phase block where the "Next round in Xs" text was replaced.

Acceptance:
- Final round reveal: visible countdown ticking down, then leaderboard appears at 0
- No regression on intermediate rounds

title: Artist prompt rejection: clearer feedback + preserve draft + specific reason
description:
From GitHub issue #92

When a player's artist prompt is rejected (moderation OR Gemini image-gen safety), the UX needs to be unambiguous:

Required:
- Their original draft must NOT be lost — they can tweak and resubmit
- They must clearly know it was rejected (not "the timer ran out")
- Tell them WHY: which moderation rule, or which safety category Gemini flagged

Current state:
- \`app/api/submit-artist-prompt/route.ts\` runs \`moderatePrompt()\` before phase flip (#73 fixed that part) and returns a JSON \`{error, detail}\` — surface \`detail\` prominently
- The reject banner exists but apparently still gets missed

Investigation/work:
- Confirm draft is restored on every reject path (moderation, image-gen, network error)
- Banner styling: bigger, top of textarea, persistent until next edit
- Include the specific reason (e.g., "Rejected: prompt mentioned violence — try another idea")
- Possibly a one-line "examples that work" link on the third reject

Acceptance:
- Manually trigger 3 rejections in a row, confirm: draft preserved each time, reason stated clearly, no time-out confusion
- Add an e2e test that submits a banned-word prompt and asserts banner + draft persistence

title: Random 'this page couldn't load' crash mid-game; reload recovers
description:
From GitHub issue #91

User reports a "this page couldn't load" Vercel error mid-game. Reload puts them back in the room.

Console preceding the crash:
\`\`\`
Realtime send() is automatically falling back to REST API. This behavior will be deprecated in the future. Please use httpSend() explicitly for REST delivery.
  at send (.../realtime-...)
  at iy (React internals)
  at uS / ub / uw (React render loop, looped many times)
\`\`\`

Two signals here:
1. **Supabase Realtime deprecation warning** — \`channel.send()\` is silently falling back to REST. Future versions of \`@supabase/supabase-js\` will require an explicit \`httpSend()\`. We should:
   - Audit every \`channel.send({type:"broadcast",...})\` call in the codebase (cursors, chat, reactions, skip-vote)
   - Either ensure the channel is SUBSCRIBED before sending (right now we may send before subscribe completes)
   - Or call \`httpSend()\` explicitly when we want REST semantics
2. **Render loop with ub/uw repeating hundreds of frames** — looks like an effect or render is re-running until React errors out. Possibly tied to an unmemoized prop / state update inside an effect that triggers itself.

Repro: open a room, play a couple rounds, watch for the page-couldn't-load error. Hard to deterministically repro without bot stress.

Acceptance:
- No "Realtime send() is automatically falling back" warnings in the console
- No page-couldn't-load crashes during a 5-round game with 8+ players

title: Chat panel should auto-scroll to most recent message on open
description:
From GitHub issue #90

When the chat panel is opened (or first mounts), it should land at the most recent message. Today it can land mid-history depending on the load order.

\`components/chat-panel.tsx\` already auto-scrolls on \`visibleMessages.length\` change (~L130-134), but on first mount the messages load via the 2s poll AFTER the panel renders, and the scroll doesn't get re-fired correctly when the panel opens (variant=floating, collapsed→open transition).

Fix: scroll to bottom on:
- First message load
- \`collapsed\` flipping to false
- Tab switch (room ↔ team)

Acceptance:
- Open chat in a 50-message room, see the latest message without manual scrolling

title: Intermittent POST /api/start-round 404 on round transition
description:
From GitHub issue #89

User intermittently sees:
\`\`\`
POST https://promptionary.io/api/start-round 404 (Not Found)
\`\`\`
followed by a render glitch / "this page couldn't load" sometimes. Reload puts them back in the game.

\`app/api/start-round/route.ts\` returns a JSON 404 in two paths:
- \`!round\` (\`round_id\` not found in DB)
- \`!room\` (room not found)

Hypothesis: stale-round race. \`/api/start-round\` errors at L189–204 delete the round on Gemini failure. If the host's tab POSTs again with that now-gone \`round_id\`, we 404. Same shape if the host has a stale phase view (their poll lagged a phase advance).

Investigation:
- Add the response body to the client-side log so we can tell "round not found" from "room not found" without server logs
- Audit the call sites in \`game-client.tsx\` that POST to start-round — are they retrying after a failure? Are they reading \`currentRound.id\` from a stale closure?
- Consider: when 404, surface a "this round was reset, waiting for next round" toast instead of a render crash

Acceptance:
- 100 rounds played, zero 404 logs (or at most 1, with a clear UX path that doesn't crash the page)

title: Round-end: numbers under each player's score don't make sense
description:
From GitHub issue #88

On the round-end screen, the right side of each player row shows their total score (clear) plus a smaller number underneath that isn't labeled and is confusing.

Need to either:
- Label it (e.g., "+12 this round" / "Round score") — preferred
- Or remove it if it's redundant

Possible candidates for what this number is:
- This round's delta
- Best guess's score
- Speed bonus
- A leftover debug field

Files: \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` — \`LeaderboardRow\` (~L1989) and the \`GuessRow\` (~L1920) reveal stack.

Acceptance:
- Every number on the round-end / leaderboard screen has a clear visual label or is removed

title: Round-end player rows: light blue fill is unreadable in dark mode
description:
From GitHub issue #87

On the round-end / reveal screen, each player's row (with what they guessed) is filled with a light blue background. In dark mode the contrast against the card text is too low — guesses are hard to read.

Likely culprit: a hard-coded \`bg-blue-100\` / \`bg-blue-200\` / \`bg-sky-*\` utility in the per-player-guess row, which doesn't flip in dark mode. AGENTS.md style rule: "No hardcoded text-white / bg-white/X / color-500 utilities in theme-aware code. Use semantic tokens."

Files to grep:
- \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` — \`GuessRow\` component (~L1920) and \`LeaderboardRow\` (~L1989)
- Possibly the recap flipboard tokens

Acceptance:
- Round-end guess rows are legible in both light and dark themes
- Replace the offending utility with a semantic token (\`bg-muted\`, \`bg-card\`, \`bg-accent\`)

title: Round-end sound sometimes plays twice
description:
From GitHub issue #86

The reveal SFX (\`playReveal\` / winner cheer) intermittently fires twice on a round transition.

Likely cause:
- Phase transition effect in \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` running a \`useEffect\` that fires on both the realtime push AND the 2s poll backstop, before the \`revealFiredRef\` guard at lines ~775 catches up
- Or React 18 strict-mode double-invocation in dev (verify it's prod-only first)

Repro: play a round to reveal, listen for double-cheer. Probably ~1 in 4 rounds.

Fix idea: guard with a \`revealFiredFor: roundId\` ref instead of a boolean — boolean ref might be reset by an unrelated render.

Acceptance:
- 5 consecutive rounds played, no audible doubled SFX

title: Add background music with phase-aware tracks
description:
From GitHub issue #85

We have one-shot SFX (\`lib/sfx.ts\`) but no background music. Game feels dry without it.

Want:
- Lobby loop — chill anticipatory
- Generating phase — tense / "AI brewing" loop
- Guessing phase — upbeat tick-tock loop
- Reveal — short jingle or stinger
- Game over — celebratory loop

Implementation thoughts:
- Royalty-free track pack (8-bit / lo-fi / kazoo-y to match the Jackbox vibe)
- Crossfade between phases (~500ms)
- One mute control covering both BGM and SFX (already in user prefs?)
- Respect \`prefers-reduced-motion\`? Or separate audio toggle.
- Probably \`<audio loop>\` with refs keyed by phase, swapped via the existing phase-transition effects in \`game-client.tsx\`.

Acceptance:
- Music plays, phase changes swap tracks with crossfade
- Mute toggles both BGM + SFX
- No autoplay-policy warnings (must trigger off first user interaction)

title: Scoreboard scrollbar with many players looks tacky
description:
From GitHub issue #84

With a full lobby (8-10 players) the running scoreboard / player rail in-game scrolls and the default browser scrollbar shows up — looks unfinished against the rest of the Jackbox-y aesthetic.

Options:
- Custom scrollbar style (\`scrollbar-width: thin\`, \`::-webkit-scrollbar\` rules with theme colors)
- Or use a horizontal pill row that wraps instead of vertical scroll
- Or fade-mask the bottom edge so it's not a hard cutoff

Files to look at:
- \`app/play/[code]/game-client.tsx\` — running scoreboard (\`leaderboard.length\` block ~L1368)
- \`app/play/[code]/lobby-client.tsx\` — player list pre-game
- Anywhere we render the player rail at game-over

Acceptance:
- 10-player room: scrollbar (if any) matches the theme in both light and dark mode, no default browser chrome visible

title: Browser console: CSP blocks the use of 'eval' in JavaScript
description:
From GitHub issue #83

Browser console reports:
\`\`\`
Content Security Policy of your site blocks the use of 'eval' in JavaScript
\`\`\`

Investigate which dependency or build path is invoking \`eval\` (or \`new Function(...)\`) so we can either remove the call or relax CSP for that specific case. Likely candidates:
- A third-party library (canvas-confetti, qrcode.react, supabase realtime, simplewebauthn) doing dynamic codegen
- Turbopack runtime in dev (shouldn't affect prod, but worth confirming)
- One of our own JSON.parse-style helpers using \`Function\` for parsing

If we can't kill the eval, audit \`next.config.*\` / response headers and add \`'unsafe-eval'\` (only in dev) or relax via nonce/hash in prod.

Acceptance:
- No CSP eval warnings in prod console on a normal /play/[code] page load

title: perf: parallelize role-tagging with image gen in party mode
description:
From GitHub issue #81

## Context
From the 2026-04-25 image-gen deep-think session. Party mode currently runs:
1. \`authorPromptWithRoles\` (returns prompt + role tokens in ONE call) → 2-4s
2. \`generateImagePng(prompt)\` → 15-40s

Artist mode already parallelizes \`Promise.all([tagPromptRoles, generateImagePng])\`. Party mode could do the same if we split authoring into two steps:
- Call A: cheap prompt-only authoring (no \`tokens\` schema field) → ~1s
- Kick off image gen immediately
- Call B: \`tagPromptRoles(prompt)\` runs in parallel with image gen

Total saved on the critical path: ~1-2s. Small but free.

## Proposal
- Add \`authorPromptOnly()\` to \`lib/gemini.ts\` — same as \`authorPromptWithRoles\` but the JSON schema has only \`{ prompt }\`.
- In \`/api/start-round\`'s party path:
  - Call \`authorPromptOnly()\` → get \`prompt\`
  - \`Promise.all([tagPromptRoles(prompt), generateImagePng(prompt)])\` → tokens + buffer
- Existing \`authorPromptWithRoles\` can stay as-is OR be removed if unused after this.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Party-mode start-round shaves 1-2s off the critical path on prod
- [ ] Token positions still match the rendered prompt
- [ ] Mock-mode parity preserved

Does NOT apply to round 2+ in party mode (those use the prefetch path which already parallelizes inside \`/api/prefetch-next-round\` — wait, double-check that). Round 1 + artist-mode rounds benefit.

title: perf: lower-res "first paint" image during generating phase
description:
From GitHub issue #80

## Context
From the 2026-04-25 image-gen deep-think session. Today players watch the loading spinner for the full duration of image gen. If we can show *something* sooner, perceived latency drops even if total wall time doesn't.

## Proposal
Two paths, pick whichever Gemini APIs actually expose:
1. **Smaller-resolution call.** Some Gemini image endpoints accept \`imageConfig: { aspectRatio, imageSize }\`. A 768x768 (or 512x512) call usually returns several seconds faster than 1024x1024. Display that, then optionally swap to a higher-res second call.
2. **Two parallel calls.** Cheap fast model (e.g. Imagen Fast at small size) AND quality model in \`Promise.all\`. Render the fast one as soon as it lands, swap when the quality one finishes. Doubles cost per round.

Path 1 is probably the right MVP. Path 2 if we want to flex.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Players see *some* image within ~5-8s of phase flip to \`generating\`
- [ ] Image appears smaller / lower-res but recognizable
- [ ] If we go with path 2: quality version replaces the fast one in-place when ready
- [ ] No regression in finalize/scoring (token positions still match the prompt that ended up rendered)

## Risks
- Token positions in \`round_prompt_tokens\` are tied to a single prompt. If we use a different model with different prompt-fidelity, the tokens still match the prompt, so scoring is fine. But guesses may map less well to a low-fidelity image.
- 2× cost on path 2.

title: perf: try Imagen 4 Fast for image generation
description:
From GitHub issue #79

## Context
Came out of the 2026-04-25 image-gen deep-think session. We shipped speculative pre-gen (#75) which makes round 2+ effectively instant in party mode, but **round 1 still costs the full 15-40s of Gemini image generation**, and artist-mode rounds can't be pre-gen'd at all.

Imagen 4 Fast typically renders in ~5-15s vs 15-40s for the current \`gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview\` (NanoBanana 2). Quality dips a little; that's the trade-off.

## Proposal
- Add \`IMAGE_MODEL=fast|quality\` env flag.
- \`lib/gemini.ts::generateImagePng\` swaps the fallback chain based on it.
- A/B in prod for a few days; eyeball recap pages for quality drop.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Flag wired through env validator
- [ ] generateImagePng honors the flag
- [ ] Round 1 + artist-mode noticeably faster on prod (median TTFI < 10s)
- [ ] Quality not visibly worse on the recap page

title: Add favorite-guess voting between rounds
description:
From GitHub issue #54

## Context

After the timer runs out in a round, all the guesses are scored and the room moves to a results screen that shows the correct answer and everyone's scores. Right now, the scores are purely algorithmic — based on how close each guess was to the real prompt and how fast it was submitted.

The user wants to let players vote on their favorite guess from the round that just ended, with the winner getting a small bonus (+10 points). This adds a social/comedy element on top of the pure accuracy scoring.

## Proposed behavior

1. **New voting phase:** After the guessing timer expires (transition from `guessing` → `scoring`), insert a short (5–10 second) vote window before revealing the scores.
2. **Who votes:** Non-artists only. The artist for that round (if artist mode is enabled) does not participate in voting, matching the existing skip-vote pattern.
3. **Ballot:** Each voter picks one guess from the round. Voters cannot vote for their own guess.
4. **Timer:** A quick countdown (5–10 seconds). When time expires, votes are tallied even if some players didn't vote.
5. **Winner determination:** The guess with the most votes wins. Ties go to one of the tied guesses (random pick or first-submitted).
6. **Score bonus:** The player who submitted the winning guess gets +10 points added to their round total.
7. **Reveal:** The vote winner is announced alongside the normal score breakdown on the results screen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A new optional phase or sub-phase appears after guessing ends and before scores are shown, allowing players to vote on their favorite guess
- [ ] Only non-artist players can cast a vote; the artist for that round (if applicable) is excluded from voting
- [ ] Players cannot vote for their own guess
- [ ] A visible countdown timer (5–10 seconds) shows how long voting is open
- [ ] When the timer expires, votes are counted even if some players didn't submit a vote
- [ ] The guess with the most votes awards +10 points to the player who wrote it
- [ ] The vote winner is displayed on the results screen alongside the normal score breakdown

## Source

https://discord.com/channels/144616492200230923/1495815432584564839

title: Add "best picture" voting at final results screen
description:
From GitHub issue #51

## Context

At the end of a game, the final results screen shows all the generated images from each round alongside the final leaderboard. Currently there's no way for players to express which image they thought was the best or most interesting. The user wants a lightweight voting mechanism that awards a cosmetic badge (chocolate chip cookie icon) to the winner(s).

## Proposed behavior

- On the final results screen, each generated image has a "Vote" button (or similar UI).
- Every player (including spectators if applicable) can cast **one** vote for their favorite image from the entire game.
- Players **can** vote for their own image.
- After voting closes (or when all players have voted), the image(s) with the most votes earn a **chocolate chip cookie icon** next to the artist's name on the leaderboard.
- **Ties:** if multiple images tie for most votes, each tied artist gets a **half-cookie icon** (or equivalent visual indicating a shared win).
- The cookie is **cosmetic only** — no score points are added.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Vote buttons appear on each image at the final results screen
- [ ] Each player can cast exactly one vote (UI prevents changing vote or voting twice)
- [ ] Self-voting is allowed
- [ ] After voting concludes, a chocolate chip cookie icon appears next to the winner's name on the leaderboard
- [ ] In the event of a tie, each tied artist receives a half-cookie (or split-cookie) icon
- [ ] The cookie award does not affect the numeric score or leaderboard ranking

## Source

https://discord.com/channels/144616492200230923/1495595714925953055

title: Add team whiteboard for waiting team during prompt-writing phase
description:
From GitHub issue #6

## Context

In team mode, while one team is writing prompts, the other team has nothing to do. Currently they just wait with no interaction. The user is asking for a shared whiteboard visible only to the waiting team (and spectators) where teammates can collaborate, brainstorm, or doodle while they wait for their turn.

## Proposed behavior

When team mode is enabled and one team is in the prompt-writing phase:

- The **waiting team** sees a shared whiteboard where they can draw or type
- Teammates see **each other's mouse cursors** on the whiteboard (color-coded per player)
- **Spectators** see the whiteboard and **all players' cursors** (both teams)
- The **prompt-writing team** does **not** see the whiteboard at all
- The whiteboard supports:
  - Drawing with multiple colored pencils
  - Typing text
  - Manual eraser tool with multiple sizes
  - Clear-all button
- The whiteboard **persists across rounds** until manually cleared or erased

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Waiting team sees a whiteboard UI during opponent's prompt-writing phase
- [ ] Whiteboard supports both drawing (with color picker) and typing
- [ ] Whiteboard includes eraser tool with size options and a clear-all button
- [ ] Teammates see only their own team's mouse cursors on the whiteboard
- [ ] Spectators see the whiteboard and all players' cursors from both teams
- [ ] Prompt-writing team does not see the whiteboard
- [ ] Whiteboard content persists across rounds until manually cleared

## Source

https://discord.com/channels/144616492200230923/1495492960702173314
